Title: Transcriptional repressor RST1 controls salt tolerance and grain yield in rice by regulating gene expression of asparagine synthetase
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream nitrogen assimilation step; asparagine-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent transfer of the amide group from glutamine to aspartate to form asparagine and glutamate; can also use ammonium as amide donor with lower efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes asparagine production, improves nitrogen utilization, reduces excess ammonium accumulation in shoots, and enhances salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in stress-associated asparagine production; not explicitly designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen storage and transport; high N:C ratio makes it suitable for long-distance transport and storage; contributes to stress adaptation under salinity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and shoots; xylem sap transports Asn from roots to shoots, while shoots accumulate high Asn under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in roots at seedling stage and in internodes and spikelets at heading stage; induced by salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in roots at seedling stage and internodes/spikelets at heading stage; upregulated in rst1 and downregulated in OsARF18 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (140 mM NaCl; also 25 mM NaCl in xylem assays); albizziin AS inhibitor; different nitrogen forms including NH4+ and NO3-
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: osas1 mutants show reduced survival under salt stress and impaired Asn accumulation; no major growth penalty from increased OsAS1 was emphasized in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsAS1 promoter in reporter assays (proAS1::LUC)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA812782; amino acid metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tos-17 insertion mutant; T-DNA insertion mutant; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; rst1 as1 double mutant analysis; enzyme activity assay in E. coli; metabolite profiling; inhibitor assay with albizziin; promoter repression assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAS1 is the direct downstream metabolic gene repressed by RST1/OsARF18. 2. Loss of OsAS1 reduces Asn accumulation, increases substrate amino acids, elevates ammonium under salt stress, and decreases survival. 3. Upregulation of OsAS1 in rst1 promotes Asn synthesis, improves N utilization, and contributes substantially to salt tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong candidate for improving salt tolerance and nitrogen-use efficiency by increasing Asn synthesis; can be combined with weakened RST1 alleles or editing of RST1 repression sites, with caution to maintain balanced nitrogen metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states OsAS1 is one of the main downstream factors of RST1; double mutant only partially suppresses rst1, indicating additional RST1 pathways may exist. E. coli assays confirmed CRISPR-truncated OsAS1 proteins were catalytically inactive.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin response factor; DNA-binding domain (DBD), repression domain (RD), PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression via promoter binding to AuxRE motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nitrogen metabolism / asparagine biosynthesis / ammonium detoxification-associated assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses OsAS1 transcription; loss of RST1 derepresses OsAS1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of salt tolerance and grain yield traits; loss of function increases Asn production, improves nitrogen utilization, lowers shoot NH4+ and Na+, raises K+, and increases grain weight/yield
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator and transcriptional brake on Asn biosynthesis through direct repression of OsAS1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen storage/transport metabolite supporting stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, shoots, and xylem-associated transport context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression in above-ground tissues than roots; expressed in root exodermis, sclerenchyma, endodermis, central cylinder, and most live cells in leaf blade and sheath; transiently induced by salt, sustainably induced by drought and ABA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in shoots than roots; salt inducible in shoots and roots
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsAS1 promoter AuxRE elements; possible PB1-mediated ARF/Aux-IAA interactions not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No direct complex experimentally established in this study
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress; drought; ABA; different nitrogen forms; field saline soil conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of RST1 improved both salt tolerance and grain yield in this study; no apparent yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native RST1 promoter for proRST1::RST1-GFP; overexpression constructs used for OsARF18-OE lines but promoter not specified; GAL4/UAS and proAS1::LUC reporter systems in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA812782; population genomics using 3K Rice Genomes Project sequences
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; T-DNA mutants; overexpression; qRT-PCR; GUS assay; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; RNA-seq; natural haplotype association; field trials
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RST1 encodes OsARF18 and acts as a transcriptional repressor localized to the nucleus. 2. RST1 directly binds AuxRE-containing regions in the OsAS1 promoter and represses its transcription. 3. Loss of RST1 elevates OsAS1 expression, promotes Asn accumulation, prevents excess shoot ammonium accumulation, improves Na+/K+ homeostasis, and increases salt tolerance and grain yield. 4. Natural haplotype variation alters repression strength, with Hap III associated with lower repression, lower shoot Na+, and higher grain weight.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for marker-assisted selection or gene editing; replacing Hap I/Hap II with superior Hap III or creating weak-loss-of-function alleles could improve salt tolerance and yield in rice and possibly other cereals
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The rst1 mutation introduces a premature stop codon at residue 330, deleting part of the repression domain and all PB1 domain. RST1 underwent directional selection during domestication and orthologs are highly conserved in grasses.
